rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KENT CHI KIN SUN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/7/20. No proof of service of notice has been filed in connection with the "Corrections" and the "CRC 7.702 Statement in Support of Amended Declaration of Counsel re Second Accounting Extraodinary Fees," etc., filed on 8/5/20. The attorney's fee declaration still does not address all of the requirements of CRC 7.702 (nature and difficulty of the tasks performed, the benefit of the services to the estate, the estimated amount of statutory compensation to be paid). The amount of fees for each of the six categories does not total $41,135.00. The math on the final page of the time sheets also appears to be incorrect. It it unclear what "SFLR 14.J" refers to. The initial Petitions for Summary Determination were denied in 2012 because they were procedurally incorrect; Judge Wiss noted at the hearing that they should have been filed as Civil actions.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authority To Engage In Couseling And Execute Any Documents Necessary To Obtain A Reverse Mortgage And To Execute Note And Deed Of Trust/Mortgage/Security Instrument And Loan Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to determine whether the proposed reverse mortgage should be approved. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Bifurcated Issue Of Probate Advance'S Entitlement To Donte Henry'S Rebate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/07/2020. Examiner notes faxed 09/02/2020 to Steven Schroeder (no email on pleading) and emailed 09/02/2020 to Konstantine Demiris. ISSUES: 1) Examiner still does not have the required courtesy copy of the Supplement filed 07/20/2020. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHRERINE ANNE PHILLIPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, subject to modification of the order with counsel's consent to require a minimum bond of $20,000, as the proposed personal representative is a nonresident of California, despite the beneficiary's bond waiver and absence of anticipated liabilities of and claims against the estate. A hearing on the status of closing the estate will be set for 12/22/2021. An appearance may be required if a petition for final distribution or for approval of a report of status of administration is not filed by 12/15/2021. No conformed copy of the signed order will be returned, as no self-addressed stamped envelope has been provided.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LARRY J STEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/20. The Acknowledgment of Receipt of Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative must set forth the proposed personal representative's address and phone number, not his attorney's. There was no codicil; it appears that the will was signed on two different dates. Although it does not appear to have been necessary, the waiver of bond attached to the petition waived bond for Lucille Geear. No courtesy copies of the proof of service or proof of publication were provided. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.93 B.] Courtesy copies of anything filed to address the issues above must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/18/20. Counsel might wish to provide a revised proposed order that has this decedent's name in the caption. (Witnesses to a will are not interested parties.)
